title: Home Maintenance and Minor Repair Services * Older Adults - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Home Maintenance and Minor Repair Services * Older Adults 6 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Older Adults Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF COMMUNITY ENERGY PROJECT WEATHERIZATION AND LEAD WORKSHOPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides workshops on basic weatherization, DIY insulation, and basic lead poisoning prevention. Qualified participants also receive a free kit of materials including items such as reusable vinyl storm window kits, door weather stripping, and pipe insulation. Materials are also available for purchase. Also offers in-home repairs and energy efficiency upgrades. 2705 E Burnside Street Suite 112 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Workshops: Unrestricted, but free supplies are restricted to households earning no more than 80% median family income In-home weatherization and small safety repairs: Age 55 and older and adults with disabilities earning no more than 80% median family income Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9:30am-5pm Workshops typically late September to early February, depending on weather and funding availability Email (503) 284-6827 108 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD HEARTS FOR SENIORS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a volunteer-based companion care program for seniors, assisting with daily tasks such as housework, meal preparation, shopping, transportation to appointments, pet care, home safety, minor repairs, and yard work. The program is volunteer-driven and professionally supported. Volunteer opportunities are available. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Companion Care: For individuals aged 65 and older who live independently in their homes and are socially isolated, with no significant cognitive impairment. Volunteers: Varies per assignment Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Companion Care: Varies per assignment Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOOD RIVER VALLEY ADULT CENTER CIRCLES OF CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a wide range of services to assist with daily needs including help with everyday chores, grocery shopping, light housework, yard work, local transportation, and friendly check-ins. Services are provided by volunteers and program accepts new volunteers's. 2010 Sterling Place Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Supportive services: Age 55 and older Volunteers: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (541) 386-2060 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH AND LAKE COUNTIES COUNCIL ON AGING HOME REPAIR PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps with minor home repairs and modifications focusing on health and safety, like ramps and heating system repairs. 404 Main Street Suite 6 Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Age 60 and older, other eligibility requirements may apply Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Email (541) 205-5400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details REACH COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT INC COMMUNITY BUILDERS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides minor home and safety repairs. Staff, volunteers, and subcontractors complete repairs as resources are available. Services may include home assessments, smoke/fire alarms, carbon monoxide detectors, bathroom safety aids, address housing code violations, yard nuisances, minor plumbing, and electrical repairs. Also assists with referrals to other home repair programs. 4150 S Moody Avenue Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: -Age 55 and older and/or adults with a permanent disability -Must own and occupy home needing the repairs -50% of area median income or less Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (503) 501-5719 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND SENIOR INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects older adults with services that allow them to remain safely in their homes and communities (elder law, home maintenance and minor repair services, housekeeping assistance, Medicare counseling, housing information and referral, advocacy, and senior ride program). Services include Oregon Project Independence Services (OPI) and community resources. Additionally offers short-term case management (Options Counseling) and chronic disease management. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Age 60 and older with a focus on the African-American community Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ENERGY PROJECT WEATHERIZATION AND LEAD WORKSHOPS HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS FOR SENIORS HOOD RIVER VALLEY ADULT CENTER CIRCLES OF CARE KLAMATH AND LAKE COUNTIES COUNCIL ON AGING HOME REPAIR PROGRAM REACH COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT INC COMMUNITY BUILDERS PROGRAM URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND SENIOR INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE
